title: Complete Ecosystem Energy Budget
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive p5.js MicroSim where students construct and balance a complete ecosystem energy budget, tracing energy from solar input through GPP, NPP, and NEP.

learningObjectives: Construct a complete energy budget tracing energy from solar input through GPP, NPP, and NEP
Explain the relationship between GPP, Ra, NPP, and NEP
Apply the first law of thermodynamics to verify energy balance in an ecosystem
Compare energy allocation patterns across different ecosystem types
prerequisites: Understanding of photosynthesis and cellular respiration
Familiarity with producers, consumers, and decomposers

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Editable flow diagram with automatic recalculation and balance checking
keyQuestions: Where does the energy go that does not become NPP?
Why is NPP always less than GPP?
What does a negative NEP value tell you about an ecosystem?
Why do only about 10% of energy transfer from one trophic level to the next?
Which ecosystem type is most efficient at storing energy?
commonMisconceptions: Energy is recycled through ecosystems like matter is
NPP and GPP are the same thing
All energy consumed by herbivores becomes herbivore biomass
assessmentOpportunities: Energy budget construction from scratch using editable values
Balance check debugging exercise to find and fix inconsistencies
Cross-ecosystem comparison table with efficiency calculations
